channel.title: MBallSectionsDR
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from MBallSectionsDR, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: States Should Put Workers First with Private Sector Labor Reforms
item.description: It’s no accident that right-to-work states have the highest net domestic population growth.

item.title: Is the Epstein Files Frenzy Turning into Trial by PDF?
item.description: LA Mayor Karen Bass’s call for Casey Wasserman to resign from the LA28 Olympic Committee reflects only panic and moral posturing.

item.title: State Department Works To Revoke Visa Of U.K. Operative Who Allegedly Censored The Federalist, Other Americans
item.description: The State Department announced its intention to revoke the visas of five foreign individuals who have allegedly censored Americans.

item.title: California Democrats Pile Out Of Clown Car At Their Convention
item.description: An all-you-can-eat buffet of free stuff and Trump sucks represented the ideological substance of California's late-AWFL Khmer Rouge party.

item.title: Conservatives, GOP lawmakers slam HuffPost critique on displays of patriotism at Olympics
item.description: Republican lawmakers and conservatives on social media are blasting a HuffPost article suggesting that it's understandable that Americans feel less pa...

item.title: Trump impeachment: The issue Democrats can't quite pick up -- or put down
item.description: Rep. Jasmine Crockett isn't shy about it -- she wants to impeach President Trump.

item.title: Fortune 500’s DEI Retreat Shows The Corporate Social Credit System Is Collapsing
item.description: Fortune 500 companies are waking up and understanding what time it is. It’s time for businesses to get back to business.

item.title: Judge blocks release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump classified documents case
item.description: A federal judge on Monday permanently barred the release of a report by special counsel Jack Smith on his investigation into President Trump's hoardin...

item.title: Surprise: Basing College Admissions On Merit Instead Of Skin Is Good For Everyone
item.description: New evidence shows that the Supreme Court’s ban on affirmative action is not a setback; instead, it facilitates improved outcomes for all.

item.title: Colorado Bill Would Take Kids Away From Parents Who Refuse To Trans Them
item.description: Colorado legislators support requiring courts to consider whether parents embrace a child’s 'gender identity' in determining custody.

item.title: Manufacturers call for clear, consistent trade framework
item.description: The National Association of Manufacturers wants a new trade framework from Congress after the Supreme Court nixed President Trump's major tariffs.

item.title: U.S. trade partners delay meetings, demand clarity after the Supreme Court guts Trump's tariff power
item.description: Nations that negotiated trade deals with President Trump are demanding clarity from the U.S. and scrutinizing their options after the Supreme Court st...

item.title: Populism Is Having a Moment in Australia
item.description: Will it last? Only if the country’s One Nation party can avoid the pitfalls experienced by other populist parties.

item.title: How Trump’s ‘Prior Authorization’ Deal Puts Patients Ahead of Paperwork
item.description: Speeding up insurers’ authorization process will deliver care more quickly and cut down on unnecessary red tape.

item.title: Don’t Prosecute Women for Abortion
item.description: While defending unborn children is essential, criminalizing women who have abortions is no way to show compassion.
